channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: NASA’s permanent Moon base plans start with three missions this year
item.description: On Tuesday, NASA announced several upcoming lunar missions to the Moon's South Pole region. These missions will pave the way for the crewed Artemis landing slated for 2028, starting with three Moon Base missions NASA says are "the first of more than a dozen missions that will be announced this year." In addition to the […]

item.title: Get ready for more bill rises — study warns data centers could hike power costs in some states over 50% by 2030
item.description: US energy supplies, largely made up of natural gas and coal, are under intense strain by data centers and other digital infrastructure.

item.title: NASA plans for up to three more lunar missions before the end of 2026
item.description: The missions will deliver payloads to the lunar surface and test equipment from Blue Origin and Astrolab.

item.title: Quote of the day by Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang: "Software is eating the world, but AI is going to eat software" — A prophetic statement predicting the impending death of software
item.description: With SaaSmageddon underway, we remember a decade-old insight into the future of the software industry

item.title: Sonos Era 300 : l’enceinte Dolby Atmos plébiscitée par la critique chute à moins de 400 €
item.description: L'enceinte connectée Sonos Era 300, pensée pour le son spatial et compatible Dolby Atmos, est aujourd'hui disponible à 391,99 euros au lieu de 459,99 euros sur Amazon. Une réduction de 68 euros sur l'un des modèles les plus ambitieux de la gamme Sonos.

item.title: Acer ProDesigner PE160WUT review: A high-res, high-refresh OLED portable monitor let down by its anti-glare display
item.description: A nifty portable USB monitor with OLED panel tech and 120 Hz refresh.

item.title: Scammers and hackers target GTA 6 fans as pre-order hype begins
item.description: As the GTA 6 release inches closer, scammers and hackers are targeting fans of the video game franchise.

item.title: OpenAI will now let your company pay to ensure 'guaranteed capacity' for up to three years
item.description: OpenAI launches 'Guaranteed Capacity' for enterprises to lock in long-term, uninterrupted compute to reduce the impacts of downtime.

item.title: Starship’s path to reusability looks murky after SpaceX’s S-1
item.description: SpaceX's recent IPO and Starship rocket test flight delivered two big data points that offer a realistic vision for the coming years — and one that may disappoint both the company's boosters and its critics.

item.title: Why the Vatican Invited Anthropic to the Pope’s AI Encyclical Presentation
item.description: Pope Leo’s first encyclical marks an unprecedented alliance between the Church and Silicon Valley.

item.title: Beware of “trophy-style” AI adoption
item.description: Companies are celebrating AI usage for its own sake, but activity isn’t the same as impact. Most enterprise generative AI investments have yet to deliver the value companies envisioned, and every day, more leaders are recognizing that people lie at the heart of the struggle. In this year’s AI & Data Leadership Executive Benchmark Survey, 93% of executives leading AI and data efforts identified human issues around culture and change management as the primary obstacle to adoption. McKinsey Global Managing Partner Bob Sternfels put it plainly on HBR’s IdeaCast: “Half if not more of the secret sa...

item.title: Pope Leo XIV’s AI encyclical is getting a mixed reception from the tech world
item.description: The Vatican’s “Magnifica Humanitas” warns about the concentration of AI power among tech elites and argues that machines can never replace human morality or consciousness. A day after Pope Leo XIV released his much anticipated encyclical on AI, the response from the AI community has been mainly positive, with some quibbling over the nature and potential of the technology....

item.title: Being a CEO “is not that complicated,” says Google CEO Sundar Pichai
item.description: In a recent interview with The Verge, Pichai said that AI is “very, very helpful in terms of decision-making” for CEOs—and only getting better. At Google, AI is reshaping employees’ titles and how they work. Last month, Google Cloud’s senior director and chief evangelist Richard Seroter told Fast Company that software engineers have turned into product engineers, or architects, as they move away from manual coding to directing teams of AI agents. It seems that AI also changed how Google’s CEO, Sundar Pichai, works, too. ...

item.title: The Best Movies to Stream This Month (May 2026)
item.description: Bugonia, Good Boy, and Send Help are among the films deserving of your eyeballs this month.
